items.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
items.summary: The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.

items.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
items.summary: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius's compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

items.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open...

items.title: Telegram’s shortlink domain is back online after day-long suspension
items.summary: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov confirmed an outage in a tweet, saying that shortlinks to the messaging app had "stopped working."

items.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
items.summary: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

items.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
items.summary: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.

items.title: David Beckham’s health drink startup IM8 takes $1B from General Catalyst’s unusual CVF fund
items.summary: General Catalyst’s Customer Value Fund doesn't make equity investments. It's providing $1 billion for IM8, known for its longevity vitamin drink.

items.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
items.summary: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.summary: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

items.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be ‘everything for everyone’
items.summary: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and...

items.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
items.summary: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

items.title: X just tweaked its algorithm to make it more friendly, less battleground
items.summary: The social media site says it will amplify posts made by users' mutual followers' to give the feed more of a communal feel.

items.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
items.summary: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.summary: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary m...

items.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

items.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
items.summary: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple...

items.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
items.summary: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

items.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
items.summary: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

items.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount’s $110B Warner Bros. deal
items.summary: The states allege that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

items.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
items.summary: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?
